name: Login Page
notes: React class component rendered by App.jsx when AuthContext.isAuthenticated === false; no client-side router is used — the login page occupies the full viewport until authentication succeeds.

Public route — no authentication guard. The only bypass is the DEV_SKIP_AUTH backend env var, which causes GET /api/health to return { skipAuth: true }; AuthProvider.checkAuthBypass() detects this and auto-authenticates before the user sees the form.

On submit, delegates entirely to AuthContext.login(email, password); does not dispatch any Redux actions — auth state lives in React context only (Redux authReducer and userReducer exist but are unused by this flow).

login() always crashes in production: it calls POST /api/auth/refresh immediately after POST /api/auth/login, but /refresh returns { session: {...} } instead of { token: '...' }, causing response.data.token.split('.') to throw TypeError (FR-007).
